**Aoftionz Live Summary**

 * **The other day, I asked NongZon (kaijeng) if he was tired.** He replied, “I haven't even started working today,” so I didn't know what to ask next. 😂

 * **NuNew’s solo concert is in the plans**, but I really can't spoil anything right now. It will definitely happen within this year. The NuNew brand will not disappoint anyone. Once it's announced, I want everyone to come.

 * **The Sports Day event** will feature both sports competitions and special shows.

 * **I really like Zee with short hair.** Just the other day, I complimented him, saying he looks younger.

 * **For #ZeeNuNewTheReality,** we want you to see sides of them that no one has ever seen before. It has to be truly real.

 * **Regarding the lightstick price:** I don't want people to compare it with Korean groups because they produce in massive quantities at a time, and normally, the higher the production volume, the cheaper the cost per unit.

 * **For all artists, Fashion Week invitations** usually come in quite last-minute. If an existing schedule can be shifted around, we will do it so the kids can attend. But if we already accepted another job beforehand, we have to fulfill that responsibility. (We also have to look at their schedules and the artists' own preferences, because they might want to focus on other work during that period).

 * **I acknowledge the feedback regarding "recasting/changing actors,"** but I cannot speak about it here.

 * **Feedback has already been sent** regarding the response times/customer service of the DMD Shop.

 * *Mon Rak Hug E-Lee* (Isan Love Spell) should be something brand new for the industry. I'm excited about it too. It's definitely going to be *muan* (fun/entertaining).

 * **I constantly teach the kids to prioritize manners and respectfulness** (being quick to *wai* and show respect), as it makes them look more endearing. During artist interviews/auditions, we look at their attitude toward fans. We regularly talk with the younger artists to keep ourselves updated and find a middle ground that suits the younger generation today.

 * **DMD's current content is highly diverse.** We want everyone to have options that fit their preferences. We are trying to experiment with new things. (I'd prefer if people didn't use the word "Cringe" so carelessly, as it might cause some people to lose confidence. Some things might just be down to personal preference).

 * **The *The Next Prince* (Khon Fah Kiang Ther) Boxset** might have a few scenes slightly re-edited.

 * **Regarding the benefit compensation,** I've already informed the team to announce it as soon as everything is finalized.

 * **Squishies?** There will (probably) be some. I want you to wait and see.

 * **Engagement from all parties is important**—whether it's from the artists, the staff, or the fans.

 * **For the Domundi Cafe,** I really want to collab with Pokémon. My life would truly be complete.

🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍

 **#P'Aof's Message to Us:**
> "I want everyone to play on X (Twitter) happily. If there's something you can overlook, you should just let it go. Follow your artists mindfully and remember why you started following them in the first place... I'm truly worried about everyone. I don't want you to read certain things and end up feeling discouraged. I really don't want it to be like that."
> 
🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍🤍

What Stays ? @Frag_Growth . 

The library was on the ground floor of the building where he grew up. His father went there for detective novels.

@jenyavyguzov went for something else.

Art books. Fashion magazines. Images from worlds he couldn't yet explain, only feel.

He was born in Krasnoyarsk, Siberia, into a working-class family. Far from the cities those magazines came from, but close enough to imagine them.

He began collecting discarded pages, cutting people, landscapes and textures into collages that gave shape to feelings he didn't yet have words for.

His family imagined a more practical future. He tried financial college, left after two years, worked as a waiter, sold advertising, and kept making art whenever life allowed him to.

Eventually, fashion took him abroad as a model.

The job paid the bills. The cities changed his life.

~ The Flower That Stayed

Travel gave Jenya something more valuable than opportunity.

It taught him to look.

Every city carried its own language cracked walls, peeling paint, worn concrete, rusted metal, forgotten surfaces shaped by years of weather and people passing through.

Most saw decay, He saw texture.

He photographed everything. Without realizing it, he was building an archive.

In 2015, a flower appeared in his work.

It has never left, Not painted, Not illustrated. BUILT. 

Every petal is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures collected across Europe and Asia. Walls become petals. Rust becomes color. Concrete becomes softness. Places that had never met find themselves blooming together.

A visual diary disguised as a flower.

Years later came Vogue, GQ Style, Tatler, Elle, Adidas, Puma, Lacoste, IKEA, exhibitions across Europe, educational lectures, and an invitation to create the official artwork for the Paris Prints Textile Show.

The recognition mattered.

But the process never changed.

He still builds the same way he always has.

One fragment at a time.

~ Fragments of Growth Is Not a just a Collection. 

It is a language built over more than a decade. Every flower is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures Jenya captured across cities and forgotten surfaces around the world. No generative AI. No algorithms. Just fragments, collected over time and composed into something entirely new.

The blockchain is simply where that journey continues. 

Through limited drops, evolving collector mechanics, and works from Jenya's personal archive, Fragments of Growth invites others into the story. Because every petal remembers a place, every texture remembers time, and every artwork proves the same quiet idea, beauty isn't created it is discovered.

@Frag_Growth is that film, one petal at a time. 🌸

Today, we're excited to release DFD (Data Forcing Distillation), a single-line fix that restores diversity and fidelity in DMD for few-step video distillation, led by my PhD student @SiyiChen277112! It improves on text-to-video, image-to-video, and causal auto-regressive video generation. 

The idea is simple: directly introducing real video samples into the training objective during distillation, so that the student can be explicitly pulled toward the diverse, high-quality real data distribution. This is implemented as a single line of code change when computing teacher scores: instead of evaluating the teacher score at the student's own generation (original DMD), we evaluate it on a real sample and compute the discrepancy with the fake sample. This pulls the student toward modes it missed (mitigating mode collapse) and away from over-saturated modes absent in real data.

With this single-line change, we can purely discard the GAN loss, and restore the diversity/fidelity by finetuning the DMD checkpoint with only 100–300 steps. 

Everything is released. Code (including both inference & training), pretrained model, and data:
Paper: https://t.co/GyWdjZ1Gah
Project page: https://t.co/8xnKb3vTKY
Code, model, and data: https://t.co/WGyo0kPMb1

We also want to have a huge shout-out to the authors of FastGen (https://t.co/SnPsjXLPX4) and Self-Forcing (https://t.co/KN3nli41oG) for releasing their code, which makes our work possible!

#VideoGeneration #DiffusionModels #AI #OpenSource

DYOR & NFA.

🌸 Alpha - Fragments of Growth

A decade of travel, memories, and textures - now brought on-chain.

Created by fashion photographer and visual artist Jenya Vyguzov, the collection transforms textures captured across years of travel into handcrafted collage flowers , each petal representing a place, memory, or moment. No AI, no generative outputs, every piece is individually composed by hand.

What stands out here is the artist behind it. Jenya has spent over a decade working with brands like Vogue, GQ Style, Tatler, Elle, Puma, and IKEA, while building a distinctive visual language. His work was even selected for the official Paris Prints Textile Show poster, where he was given an entire floor for a solo exhibition.

⚠️WL applications are coming soon. If you're interested in art NFTs, this is the stage where you want to be paying attention. Follow the artist and the project, turn on notifications, and be ready when WL applications open.

🔎 Mint Details TBA

dyor/nfa

Diffusion language models, which generate text by repeatedly filling in masked parts rather than writing strictly from left to right, are becoming a serious alternative to standard transformer language models, but the usual ways of improving reasoning after training were built for left-to-right generation and do not fit the diffusion models.

The authors of this paper introduce a self-training method in which the model first produces its own answer, then uses parts of that completed answer as helpful context while learning how earlier denoising steps should behave.

Instead of comparing teacher and student one next token at a time, it compares them at each denoising step, which matches how diffusion language models actually generate text. 

Across math and planning tasks, d-OPSD matches or beats supervised finetuning and reinforcement-learning baselines, while needing far fewer optimization steps than the main RL baseline.

https://t.co/eRqYj4YPwi

🎥 This is by far one of my best AI works in fashion and editorial filmmaking 🎬 

🖤 I've had this avant-garde fashion film concept for a very long time. Outdoor. Heavy avant-garde clothing. Dark, moody atmosphere. A visual world I've always wanted to bring to life — but was waiting for the right tools to make it exactly the way I imagined it.

✨ When I tested the new image generation models a few days ago, I immediately knew — the time is now. And I didn't find any better platform than @invideoOfficial to bring this to life 🌼
